findings.evidence: <main> at line 27 contains another <main> at line 57. Only one <main> landmark is valid per document.

findings.description: Ensure the main landmark is at top level
findings.evidence:
Fix any of the following:
  The main landmark is contained in another landmark. [<main>]

findings.description: Ensure the document has at most one main landmark
findings.evidence:
Fix any of the following:
  Document has more than one main landmark [<main>]

Fix any of the following:
  The landmark must have a unique aria-label, aria-labelledby, or title to make landmarks distinguishable [<main>]

findings.description: Ensure that the page, or at least one of its frames contains a level-one heading
findings.evidence:
Fix all of the following:
  Page must have a level-one heading [<html lang="en">]

findings.description: Authorized signature image/handwritten signature is missing from the HTML conversion
findings.evidence: PDF page 1 shows a handwritten signature next to 'AUTHORIZED SIGNATURE' field and another signature in the 'FOR DENR USE ONLY' section. The HTML replaces these with '[Signature]' text placeholder.

findings.description: The vendor information form layout is converted from a visually distinct form layout to a three-column table structure that introduces redundant column headers and duplicates data
findings.evidence: PDF page 1 shows a two-column form layout (labels on left, values on right). The HTML converts this into a three-column table with 'Column 1', 'Column 2', 'Column 3' headers, where some data like Federal ID and FAX NUMBER appear duplicated across columns 2 and 3.

findings.description: The vendor information table on page 1 is restructured into a 3-column table in HTML, but the PDF shows a 2-column layout. The FEDERAL ID OR SOCIAL SECURITY NO. value '52-1413880' is duplicated across columns 2 and 3, and the FAX NUMBER '919-831-1295' is similarly duplicated. The TOLL FREE TEL. NO field is present but shown as its own column when it was part of the same row as TELEPHONE NUMBER in the PDF.
findings.evidence: PDF: Two-column layout with FEDERAL ID in one merged cell. HTML: <th>Column 1</th><th>Column 2</th><th>Column 3</th> with duplicated values like '52-1413880' appearing in both Column 2 and Column 3.

findings.description: Exhibit 2 heading and substantial content from Exhibit 2 (Moffatt & Nichol scope of work) is missing as a clearly identified section. The Exhibit 2 content appears partially in the HTML without the 'Exhibit 2' heading. PDF pages 8-9 contain Exhibit 2 content including sections on scope, agricultural demand methodology, and deliverables, but the HTML lacks the 'Exhibit 2' heading.
findings.evidence: PDF page 9 has content from Exhibit 2 including crop irrigation methodology, municipal/industrial withdrawal computation, deliverables section (2.0), schedule (3.0), and estimate of cost (4.0). HTML includes the content but never labels it as 'Exhibit 2' - only Exhibit 1 and Exhibit 3 have explicit headings.

findings.description: Pages 2-3 of the PDF contain numbered instructions/conditions (items 1-13 approximately) under various headings. Some of these items from pages 2-6 in the PDF appear to be present but are reorganized and some instructional content from the beginning of page 3 (documentation for protest, item 14 MISCELLANEOUS) appears mixed with ITS Terms content.
findings.evidence: PDF page 3 top has protest documentation text and item 14 MISCELLANEOUS. In the HTML, this content appears within the ITS Terms section rather than in a separate instructions section, though the text itself is present.

findings.description: The HTML states 'Offer accepted and contract awarded this 1 day of JANUARY, 2008' but the PDF handwriting appears to show 'JANUARY' (partially legible). The '2008' in the HTML appears to be read as '200[8]' from the PDF which shows '200' followed by a handwritten character that could be interpreted as '8'. This is a reasonable interpretation of handwritten text, not fabrication.
findings.evidence: PDF: handwritten 'day of JANUARY, 200[handwritten]'. HTML: '1 day of JANUARY, 2008'. The PDF shows what appears to be a handwritten 'B' or '8' after '200'.
